meta.name: CFS Chalder Fatigue Scale
meta.description: The Chalder Fatigue Scale is an 11-item questionnaire measuring the severity of physical and mental fatigue. It is widely used in clinical and research settings to assess fatigue in various populations.
meta.author: Chalder, Berelowitz, Pawlikowska, Watts, Wessely, Wright & Wallace (1993)
meta.reference: Chalder T, Berelowitz G, Pawlikowska T, Watts L, Wessely S, Wright D, Wallace EP. Development of a fatigue scale. J Psychosom Res. 1993;37(2):147-153.
meta.instruction: We would like to know more about any problems you have had with feeling tired, weak, or lacking in energy in the last month. Please answer ALL the questions by selecting the answer which applies to you most closely.

questions.text: Do you have problems with tiredness?
Do you need to rest more?
Do you feel sleepy or drowsy?
Do you have problems starting things?
Do you have less strength in your muscles?
Do you have problems thinking clearly?
Do you make slips of the tongue when speaking?

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your fatigue levels are within the normal range, with no clinically significant physical or mental fatigue detected. Your energy levels appear adequate for managing daily activities and responsibilities effectively.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good sleep hygiene with a consistent sleep schedule of seven to nine hours per night
Keep up regular physical activity and a balanced diet to sustain your current energy levels
Monitor your energy levels over time and take note of any changes in fatigue patterns

ranges.label: Mild Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing mild fatigue that may slightly affect your daily functioning and productivity. Both physical stamina and mental sharpness may be somewhat reduced compared to your usual baseline.
suggestions: Ensure you are consistently getting adequate sleep of seven to nine hours each night with a regular bedtime routine
Review your daily schedule to identify opportunities for short rest breaks that can help restore energy
Incorporate light to moderate exercise such as walking or stretching to naturally boost your energy levels
If fatigue persists for more than two weeks, consult a healthcare provider to rule out underlying medical causes

ranges.label: Moderate Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing moderate fatigue that is likely affecting your work performance, social activities, and overall quality of life. Both physical endurance and cognitive functioning may be noticeably compromised.
suggestions: Consult a healthcare provider to investigate potential medical causes such as thyroid dysfunction, anemia, or sleep disorders
Implement energy management and pacing strategies by alternating periods of activity with scheduled rest throughout the day
Prioritize essential tasks and delegate or postpone less critical responsibilities to conserve your limited energy
Review all current medications and lifestyle factors with your doctor to identify contributors to your fatigue
Consider keeping a fatigue diary to track patterns and identify triggers that worsen your symptoms

ranges.label: Severe Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing severe fatigue that is likely significantly impairing your daily functioning, work capacity, and quality of life. This level of exhaustion warrants prompt medical evaluation.
suggestions: Seek medical evaluation promptly to identify and address any underlying physical or psychological causes of your fatigue
Discuss comprehensive treatment options with your healthcare provider, including medication review and sleep assessment
Practice strict energy conservation and pacing to prevent post-exertional worsening of your symptoms
Consider psychological support if your fatigue is linked to chronic stress, mood disorders, or burnout

physical.label: Normal Physical Fatigue
physical.description: Your physical fatigue is within the normal range. Your body energy reserves and physical stamina appear adequate for daily demands.

physical.label: Mild Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing mild physical fatigue. Tiredness, muscle weakness, or reduced stamina may be present but remain manageable with adequate rest.

physical.label: Moderate Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing notable physical fatigue that may be limiting your physical activities and endurance. Consider consulting a healthcare provider.

physical.label: Severe Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing severe physical fatigue that significantly limits your physical functioning. Medical evaluation is recommended to identify causes.

mental.label: Normal Mental Fatigue
mental.description: Your mental fatigue is within the normal range. Your concentration, memory, and cognitive clarity appear to be functioning well.

mental.label: Mild Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing mild mental fatigue. Occasional difficulty with concentration or word-finding may occur but does not significantly impair functioning.

mental.label: Moderate Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing notable mental fatigue that may be affecting your concentration, memory, and ability to think clearly during daily tasks.

mental.label: Severe Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing severe mental fatigue with significant cognitive impairment. Professional evaluation is recommended to address these symptoms.
